question.title: ModelMapper throws an error when maps one List to Map but works correctly with another List that is actually the same
question.body:
I am using ModelMapper v3.1.0 for my project on SpringBoot v2.7.4.

There are three classes: DoctorEntity, Doctor(Model) and DoctorDTO:

public class Doctor {
    // Key is ID of the value
    private Map<Long, AppointmentInterface> appointments;
    ...
}

public class DoctorDTO {
    private List<AppointmentDto> appointments;
    ...
}

public class DoctorEntity {
    private List<AppointmentEntity> appointmentEntities
    ...
}

As you can see Doctor's property is Map, while others are Lists.

To help ModelMapper map 'List' into 'Map' I had to override the methods:
DoctorDTO -> Doctor:

@Override
protected void mapSpecificFieldsToModelFromDto(DoctorDto source, Doctor destination) {

    if (source.getAppointments() != null)
        destination.setAppointments(source.getAppointments()
                .stream()
                .collect(Collectors.toMap(AppointmentDto::getId,
                        elem -> mapper.map(elem, Appointment.class))));
}

DoctorEntity -> Doctor:

@Override
protected void mapSpecificFieldsToModelFromEntity(DoctorEntity source, Doctor destination) {

    if (source.getAppointmentEntities() != null)
        destination.setAppointments(source.getAppointmentEntities()
                .stream()
                .collect(Collectors.toMap(AppointmentEntity::getId,
                        elem -> mapper.map(elem, Appointment.class))));
}

I have written two tests to check the mapping to Doctor:

1(Passes):

@Autowired
private Mapper<DoctorEntity, Doctor, DoctorDto> doctorMapper;

@Test
void mapEntityToModel() {
    DoctorEntity doctorEntity = new DoctorEntity(1L,
            ...
            List.of(new AppointmentEntity(1L,
                            LocalDate.now(),
                            AppointmentState.OPEN,
                            null,
                            null,
                            null)
            ));

    Doctor doctor = doctorMapper.toModelFromEntity(doctorEntity);


    doctorEntity.getAppointments().forEach(a -> assertTrue(doctor.getAppointments().containsKey(a.getId())));
}

2(Fails):

@Autowired
private Mapper<DoctorEntity, Doctor, DoctorDto> doctorMapper;

void mapDtoToModel() {
        DoctorDto doctorDto = new DoctorDto(1L,
                ...
                List.of(new AppointmentDto(1L,
                                LocalDate.now(),
                                AppointmentState.OPEN,
                                null,
                                null,
                                null)
                ));

        Doctor doctor = doctorMapper.toModelFromDto(doctorDto);

        doctorDto.getAppointments().forEach(a -> assertTrue(doctor.getAppointments().containsKey(a.getId())));
    }

The issue occurs when ModelMapper tries to map DoctorDTO to Doctor but throws an error:

1) Failed to instantiate instance of destination java.util.Map. Ensure that java.util.Map has a non-private no-argument constructor.

However, it maps DoctorEntity to Doctor correctly without throwing any error.

I've tried to change Map interface to HashMap and it did the trick. But I can't understand why does ModelMapper maps one List to Map and doesn't map the other when they're the same.
